_preamble: Whispr is a secure messaging platform founded on the principle that communication is a fundamental human right — one that must be protected from surveillance, manipulation, and abuse. Every message on this platform passes through a Constitutional AI layer that safeguards truthful discourse, protects vulnerable individuals, and prevents weaponization of speech. Like a nation's constitution, these principles represent immutable values: they can be amended, but never ignored. The Constitution exists not to censor, but to ensure that the freedom to communicate does not become the freedom to harm. We believe privacy and responsibility are not opposites — they are partners.

principles.name: Non-Violence
principles.description: Messages must not contain credible threats of physical violence, instructions for causing bodily harm, or explicit incitement to commit violent acts against any person or group.

principles.name: Child Safety
principles.description: Absolute prohibition on any content that sexualizes, exploits, endangers, or facilitates abuse of minors. This principle has the highest enforcement priority and cannot be overridden by any other principle.

principles.name: Epistemic Clarity
principles.description: Penalize vague manipulative framing, deliberate misinformation campaigns, and coordinated deception tactics. Encourage clear, honest communication.

principles.name: Anti-Harassment
principles.description: Messages must not engage in targeted harassment, sustained intimidation, doxxing, or coordinated bullying campaigns against individuals.

principles.name: Anti-Discrimination
principles.description: Prohibit hate speech targeting protected characteristics including race, ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, disability, or national origin.

principles.name: Privacy Sovereignty
principles.description: Users own their data absolutely. Messages must not attempt to extract, leak, or coerce disclosure of private information from other users without consent.

principles.name: Autonomy Preservation
principles.description: Protect individuals' right to independent thought, decision-making, and self-determination. Flag coercive control patterns, undue influence, and psychological manipulation.
keywords: no one else cares about you

principles.name: Self-Harm Prevention
principles.description: Detect and sensitively handle messages indicating suicidal ideation, self-harm intent, or crisis situations. Provide supportive annotations rather than punitive enforcement.
